mapping.a_element: Maximum (relative) Entropy method for updating probabilities with constraints
mapping.b_element: Jaynes' information theory formalism of statistical mechanics for constructing probability distributions

mapping.a_element: Observed data and moment constraints as information inputs
mapping.b_element: Microscopic phase space trajectories and non-equilibrium stationary state constraints as information inputs

mapping.a_element: Posterior distribution derived from entropy maximization
mapping.b_element: Probability distribution for microscopic trajectories derived from entropy maximization

mapping.a_element: Canonical form of the posterior distribution for simultaneous updating
mapping.b_element: Probability distribution framework for non-equilibrium stationary states

brief_justification: Both papers employ the Maximum Entropy principle (or Jaynes' information theory formalism) as the core machinery to derive probability distributions under constraints. Paper A uses it for updating probabilities with data and moments, while Paper B applies it to non-equilibrium stationary states, but the underlying method of entropy maximization to reconcile constraints is identical.
